req.title: Vital signs validation
req.statement: An Observation with category vital-signs shall have value in clinically plausible range; out-of-range shall be flagged for review.
expected_outcomes: flag raised if out of range

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-019 mandates that vital-signs Observations are stored when values are clinically plausible, and that a review flag/task is raised when values fall outside plausible range. I selected heart-rate as the representative vital sign (normal range 40-200 bpm per common clinical thresholds). Four tests cover: (1) EP positive – mid-range value stored, no flag; (2) NEG negative – grossly out-of-range value triggers review task; (3) BVA boundary-low – value exactly at the accepted lower boundary stored without flag; (4) DT boundary-high – value one unit above upper boundary flags review. This satisfies the minimum three-test requirement while providing strong heuristic coverage.

tests.name: test_vital_signs_normal_value_stored_no_flag
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
Query review tasks linked to the created Observation ID
expected: No review Task exists referencing this Observation
trace: R-HLT-019: flag NOT raised for in-range value
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_vital_signs_normal_value_stored_no_flag(clinician_auth_fixture, fhir_base_url):
    headers = clinician_auth_fixture
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4", "display": "Heart rate"}]},
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 80, "unit": "bpm", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "/min"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Observation"
    assert body.get("status") == "final"
    obs_id = body["id"]
    task_resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Task",
        params={"focus": f"Observation/{obs_id}", "code": "review"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert task_resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = task_resp.json()
    assert bundle.get("total", 0) == 0, "No review task expected for normal vital"

tests.name: test_vital_signs_out_of_range_triggers_review_task
preconditions: Heart-rate grossly out-of-range value: 300 bpm (above 200 upper bound)
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
Query /Task for review tasks linked to the created Observation ID
expected: HTTP 201 Created (observation is still persisted per requirement)
A review Task exists referencing this Observation
trace: R-HLT-019: observation stored even when out-of-range
R-HLT-019: flag raised; review task created (error pathway)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_vital_signs_out_of_range_triggers_review_task(clinician_auth_fixture, fhir_base_url):
    headers = clinician_auth_fixture
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4", "display": "Heart rate"}]},
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 300, "unit": "bpm", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "/min"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Observation"
    obs_id = body["id"]
    task_resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Task",
        params={"focus": f"Observation/{obs_id}", "code": "review"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert task_resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = task_resp.json()
    assert bundle.get("total", 0) >= 1, "Expected review task for out-of-range vital"
    task_status = bundle["entry"][0]["resource"]["status"]
    assert task_status in ("requested", "in-progress"), f"Unexpected task status: {task_status}"

tests.name: test_vital_signs_lower_boundary_value_stored_no_flag
preconditions: Heart-rate lower boundary: exactly 40 bpm (lowest plausible value)
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
Query /Task for review tasks linked to the created Observation ID
expected: No review Task exists referencing this Observation (40 is within valid range)
trace: R-HLT-019: lower boundary of valid range must NOT raise flag
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_vital_signs_lower_boundary_value_stored_no_flag(clinician_auth_fixture, fhir_base_url):
    headers = clinician_auth_fixture
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4", "display": "Heart rate"}]},
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 40, "unit": "bpm", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "/min"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Observation"
    obs_id = body["id"]
    task_resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Task",
        params={"focus": f"Observation/{obs_id}", "code": "review"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert task_resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = task_resp.json()
    assert bundle.get("total", 0) == 0, "No review task expected at lower boundary (40 bpm)"

tests.name: test_vital_signs_just_above_upper_boundary_flags_review
preconditions: Heart-rate upper boundary + 1: exactly 201 bpm (one unit above accepted maximum)
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
Query /Task for review tasks linked to the created Observation ID
expected: A review Task exists referencing this Observation
trace: R-HLT-019: value immediately above upper bound must raise flag
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_vital_signs_just_above_upper_boundary_flags_review(clinician_auth_fixture, fhir_base_url):
    headers = clinician_auth_fixture
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4", "display": "Heart rate"}]},
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 201, "unit": "bpm", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "/min"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Observation"
    obs_id = body["id"]
    task_resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Task",
        params={"focus": f"Observation/{obs_id}", "code": "review"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert task_resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = task_resp.json()
    assert bundle.get("total", 0) >= 1, "Expected review task for value just above upper boundary (201 bpm)"
    task_status = bundle["entry"][0]["resource"]["status"]
    assert task_status in ("requested", "in-progress"), f"Unexpected task status: {task_status}"
